= Discussion Templates
:description: Structured community discussions for paint-type

This directory contains GitHub discussion templates that provide structure and
context for community conversations in the paint-type repository.

== Purpose

Discussion templates help:

* *Organize conversations* by topic and purpose
* *Provide context* for community members joining discussions
* *Guide participants* on what information to include
* *Maintain quality* of community interactions
* *Make discussions* more productive and focused

Unlike issues (which track work), discussions are for open-ended conversations,
brainstorming, and community collaboration.

== Contents by Functional Grouping

=== Discussion Category Templates

* `q-and-a.yml`:: Template for Question & Answer discussions. Used for:
  - Technical questions
  - Usage questions
  - Troubleshooting
  - General inquiries
  
  Collects information about:
  - The specific question or problem
  - What has been tried
  - Environment or context
  - Relevant code or examples

* `ideas.yml`:: Template for Ideas discussions. Used for:
  - Feature brainstorming
  - Architecture discussions
  - Proposal feedback
  - Future direction exploration
  
  Collects information about:
  - The idea or proposal
  - Motivation and use cases
  - Potential approaches
  - Considerations and tradeoffs

== Purpose of Each Template

=== q-and-a.yml

The Q&A template structures technical and usage discussions to:

* Clearly state the question or problem
* Provide necessary context
* Help community members provide accurate answers
* Make discussions searchable and reusable

Well-structured Q&A discussions become a knowledge base for the community.

=== ideas.yml

The Ideas template structures feature and architecture discussions to:

* Clearly articulate the proposal
* Explain the motivation and value
* Explore different approaches
* Identify potential issues or concerns
* Build consensus within the community

Good idea discussions lead to better project decisions and roadmaps.

== Discussion vs. Issues

Use *Discussions* for:

* Open-ended questions
* Brainstorming and idea exploration
* Community feedback and opinions
* General conversations

Use *Issues* for:

* Bug reports
* Feature requests (formal)
* Documentation requests
* Actionable work items

== Template Structure

All discussion templates follow a consistent structure:

1. *Category*: The discussion category (Q&A, Ideas, etc.)
2. *Title*: Clear, descriptive template name
3. *Description*: What the template is for and when to use it
4. *Labels*: Automatic labels to apply
5. *Body*: The form fields to collect information

== Best Practices

When creating or modifying discussion templates:

* Keep templates focused on specific discussion types
* Provide clear guidance on what belongs in each category
* Include helpful descriptions and examples
* Make it easy for participants to provide necessary context
* Encourage constructive and respectful conversations
